description: Direct income support of ₹6,000 per year to small and marginal farmers with cultivable land. Amount transferred in three installments of ₹2,000 each directly to bank accounts.
eligibility: All landholding farmers with cultivable land. Excludes institutional landholders, farmer families holding constitutional posts, serving/retired officers, income tax payers.
benefits: ₹6,000 per year in 3 installments of ₹2,000 each directly to bank account
application_process: Register at pmkisan.gov.in or through Common Service Centre or local agriculture department

description: Financial inclusion scheme providing basic banking services to unbanked citizens. Includes zero balance account, RuPay debit card, accidental insurance and overdraft facility.
eligibility: Any Indian citizen above 10 years of age without a bank account
benefits: Zero balance savings account, RuPay debit card, ₹2 lakh accidental insurance, ₹30,000 life cover, ₹10,000 overdraft facility
application_process: Visit nearest bank branch with Aadhaar and one passport photo

ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
description: World's largest health insurance scheme providing ₹5 lakh health coverage per family per year for secondary and tertiary hospitalization. Covers 1500+ medical procedures.
eligibility: Families identified in SECC 2011 data. BPL families, SC/ST households, rural landless households, households with no adult member, disabled member households.
benefits: ₹5 lakh health insurance per family per year, cashless treatment at empanelled hospitals
application_process: Check eligibility at pmjay.gov.in or visit nearest Common Service Centre with Aadhaar

description: Provides loans up to ₹10 lakh to non-corporate, non-farm small/micro enterprises. Three categories: Shishu (up to ₹50,000), Kishor (₹50,000 to ₹5 lakh), Tarun (₹5 lakh to ₹10 lakh).
eligibility: Any Indian citizen with a business plan for non-farm income generating activities. Small businesses, shopkeepers, artisans, vendors.
benefits: Loans up to ₹10 lakh without collateral. Shishu: up to ₹50,000. Kishor: ₹50,000-₹5 lakh. Tarun: ₹5-10 lakh.
application_process: Apply at nearest bank, MFI or NBFC with business plan and KYC documents

description: Housing scheme for rural BPL families to construct pucca houses. Provides financial assistance for construction of basic houses with toilet, LPG connection and electricity.
eligibility: Homeless rural families and those living in zero, one or two room kutcha houses. Priority to SC/ST, minorities, disabled, women.
benefits: ₹1.20 lakh in plain areas, ₹1.30 lakh in hilly/difficult areas for house construction. Additional ₹12,000 for toilet under Swachh Bharat.
application_process: Apply through Gram Panchayat or Block Development Officer. Registration at pmayg.nic.in

ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
description: Housing for All mission for urban areas providing interest subsidy on home loans for EWS, LIG and MIG categories. Credit Linked Subsidy Scheme (CLSS) component.
eligibility: EWS (income up to ₹3 lakh), LIG (₹3-6 lakh), MIG-I (₹6-12 lakh), MIG-II (₹12-18 lakh). Beneficiary should not own a pucca house.
benefits: Interest subsidy of 6.5% for EWS/LIG on loans up to ₹6 lakh. MIG-I: 4% on ₹9 lakh. MIG-II: 3% on ₹12 lakh.
application_process: Apply at pmaymis.gov.in or through empanelled banks/HFCs

scheme_name: Pradhan Mantri Jeevan Jyoti Bima Yojana (PMJJBY)
description: Life insurance scheme offering ₹2 lakh life cover at extremely low premium of ₹330 per year. Renewable annually. Covers death due to any cause.
eligibility: Age group 18-50 years with a savings bank account. Auto-debit of premium from account.
benefits: ₹2 lakh life insurance cover for just ₹330 per year (less than ₹1 per day)
application_process: Enroll through your bank account. Submit consent-cum-declaration form at bank branch.

description: Accidental insurance scheme providing ₹2 lakh cover for accidental death/permanent disability at just ₹20 per year. Renewed annually.
eligibility: Age group 18-70 years with a savings bank account
benefits: ₹2 lakh for accidental death or permanent total disability. ₹1 lakh for partial permanent disability. Premium only ₹20/year.
application_process: Enroll through bank account. Submit consent form at bank branch or through net banking.

description: Pension scheme for unorganized sector workers guaranteeing minimum monthly pension of ₹1,000 to ₹5,000 after age 60. Government co-contributes 50% of total contribution.
eligibility: Indian citizens aged 18-40 years with a savings bank account. Not covered under any statutory social security scheme.
benefits: Guaranteed monthly pension of ₹1,000/₹2,000/₹3,000/₹4,000/₹5,000 after age 60. Government contributes 50% up to ₹1,000/year for 5 years.
application_process: Apply at bank branch or post office. Available through net banking and mobile banking.

description: One-stop platform for various central government scholarships for students from pre-matric to post-doctoral level. Covers scholarships for minorities, SC/ST, OBC, disabled students.
eligibility: Students at various educational levels. Specific eligibility varies by scholarship. Generally for students with family income below ₹2.5 lakh to ₹8 lakh.
benefits: Scholarships ranging from ₹1,000/month to ₹20,000/month depending on course and category
application_process: Register at scholarships.gov.in. Apply online with academic certificates, income certificate, bank account details.

ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
description: Micro-credit scheme for street vendors to resume livelihoods affected by COVID-19. Provides collateral-free working capital loans with interest subsidy.
eligibility: Urban street vendors with Certificate of Vending or Letter of Recommendation from Urban Local Body
benefits: Initial loan of ₹10,000, enhanced to ₹20,000 then ₹50,000 on timely repayment. 7% interest subsidy.
application_process: Apply at pmsvanidhi.mohua.gov.in or through nearest bank/MFI/NBFC

description: Small savings scheme for girl child to meet future education and marriage expenses. High interest rate scheme with tax benefits under Section 80C.
eligibility: Girl child below 10 years of age. Account can be opened by parent or legal guardian. Maximum 2 accounts per family.
benefits: High interest rate (currently 8.2% per annum). Tax deduction up to ₹1.5 lakh under 80C. Maturity after 21 years from account opening.
application_process: Open account at post office or authorized bank branch with girl child birth certificate and guardian ID.

ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development
description: Scheme to address declining child sex ratio and promote welfare of girl child. Focuses on survival, protection, education and empowerment of girls.
eligibility: Girl children and their families. Special focus on districts with low child sex ratio.
benefits: Financial incentives for girl child education, conditional cash transfers, community mobilization support
application_process: Contact local Anganwadi center, health department or district administration

scheme_name: MGNREGS (Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee)
description: Guarantees 100 days of wage employment per financial year to every rural household willing to do unskilled manual work. Provides livelihood security to rural poor.
eligibility: Any adult member of rural household willing to do unskilled manual work. Must register at local Gram Panchayat.
benefits: 100 days of guaranteed wage employment. Current wage rates vary by state (₹200-350/day). Unemployment allowance if work not provided within 15 days.
application_process: Register at Gram Panchayat with Aadhaar and bank account. Job card issued within 15 days.

ministry: Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food and Public Distribution
description: Free foodgrain scheme providing 5 kg of rice/wheat per person per month free of cost to beneficiaries under National Food Security Act.
eligibility: NFSA beneficiaries — Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) and Priority Households (PHH) with ration card
benefits: 5 kg free foodgrain (rice/wheat) per person per month free of cost
application_process: Access through existing ration card at Fair Price Shop. If no ration card, apply at district food office.

ministry: Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas
description: Provides free LPG connections to women from BPL households. Aims to replace unclean cooking fuels with clean LPG. Deposit-free connections with first refill subsidized.
eligibility: Women above 18 years from BPL households. SECC-2011 or AAY/PMAY beneficiaries. SC/ST households.
benefits: Free LPG connection (cylinder + regulator + hose), deposit waiver, first refill subsidized. Up to 12 subsidized refills per year.
application_process: Apply at nearest LPG distributor with BPL certificate/Aadhaar/ration card

description: Bank loans between ₹10 lakh to ₹1 crore for SC/ST and women entrepreneurs to set up greenfield enterprises in manufacturing, services or trading sector.
eligibility: SC/ST and women entrepreneurs above 18 years setting up greenfield enterprise. At least one SC/ST and one woman borrower per bank branch.
benefits: Bank loan ₹10 lakh to ₹1 crore (75% of project cost). Repayment period up to 7 years. Working capital through overdraft/CC.
application_process: Apply at standupmitra.in or nearest bank branch with project report

description: Social security for BPL elderly, widows and disabled persons. Includes Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension, Widow Pension, and Disability Pension schemes.
eligibility: BPL households. Old age pension: 60+ years. Widow pension: 40-79 years widows. Disability pension: severely disabled persons.
benefits: Monthly pension: ₹200-500 for elderly, ₹300 for widows, ₹300 for disabled. States add their own contribution.
application_process: Apply at Gram Panchayat or Block Development Officer with age/BPL/disability certificate

ministry: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
description: Skill certification scheme enabling youth to take up industry-relevant skill training. Provides short duration skill training with monetary reward on certification.
eligibility: Indian nationals, school/college dropout or unemployed youth. No minimum education qualification for most courses.
benefits: Free skill training, ₹8,000 average monetary reward post certification, placement assistance, recognition of prior learning
application_process: Register at pmkvyofficial.org or visit nearest PMKVY Training Centre

ministry: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT)
description: Flagship initiative to build strong startup ecosystem in India. Provides tax benefits, self-certification compliance, fast-track patent examination and funding support.
eligibility: Entity incorporated as private limited company, partnership or LLP. Less than 10 years old. Annual turnover below ₹100 crore. Working on innovative product/service.
benefits: 3-year income tax exemption, 80% rebate on patent fees, self-certification for 9 labor laws, faster IP registration, INDEx fund access
application_process: Register at startupindia.gov.in with incorporation certificate and business description

description: National database of unorganized workers. Registration provides UAN number, accidental insurance under PMSBY, and helps access social security benefits.
eligibility: Unorganized sector workers aged 16-59 not covered under EPFO/ESIC/NPS. Includes farmers, construction workers, domestic workers, street vendors.
benefits: ₹2 lakh accidental death/disability insurance, UAN number for portability of benefits, priority for government welfare schemes
application_process: Register at eshram.gov.in or Common Service Centre with Aadhaar and mobile number

ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
description: Holistic support to artisans and craftspeople (Vishwakarmas) working with hands and tools in 18 trades. Provides skill training, toolkits, loans and market linkage.
eligibility: 18 traditional trades including blacksmith, goldsmith, potter, carpenter, tailor, cobbler, barber, washerman, mason, weaver, etc.
benefits: ₹15,000 toolkit incentive, skill training with ₹500/day stipend, collateral-free loan ₹1 lakh (1st tranche) and ₹2 lakh (2nd tranche) at 5% interest
application_process: Register at pmvishwakarma.gov.in through Common Service Centre with Aadhaar

description: Provides farmers with affordable credit for agricultural needs. Covers crop cultivation, post-harvest expenses, farm maintenance, allied activities and consumption needs.
eligibility: All farmers — individual/joint borrowers, tenant farmers, sharecroppers, oral lessees. Also fishermen and animal husbandry farmers.
benefits: Revolving credit up to ₹3 lakh at 4% interest (with 2% government interest subvention + 3% incentive for timely repayment). Higher limits based on landholding.
application_process: Apply at nearest bank branch, cooperative bank or regional rural bank with land documents

description: Provides farmers with soil health cards containing crop-wise recommendations for nutrients and fertilizers for individual farms to improve productivity.
eligibility: All farmers with agricultural land
benefits: Free soil testing, soil health card with recommendations, crop-wise nutrient recommendations, guidance on fertilizer use to reduce costs
application_process: Apply at nearest agriculture department office or Krishi Vigyan Kendra

description: Crop insurance scheme providing financial support to farmers suffering crop loss due to natural calamities, pests and diseases. Very low premium for farmers.
eligibility: All farmers including sharecroppers and tenant farmers growing notified crops. Mandatory for loanee farmers, voluntary for others.
benefits: Full insured sum without any upper limit. Premium: 2% for Kharif, 1.5% for Rabi, 5% for horticultural crops. Government pays remaining premium.
application_process: Apply at nearest bank, CSC, insurance company or through crop insurance app. Enroll before cutoff date for each season.

ministry: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
description: Promotes apprenticeship training by sharing 25% of stipend cost with employers. Helps youth get on-the-job training and industry experience with stipend.
eligibility: Youth aged 14+ who have passed Class 5 or above. Different categories based on qualification level.
benefits: Monthly stipend (government shares 25% up to ₹1,500/month), NAPS certification, industry experience, pathway to employment
application_process: Register at apprenticeshipindia.org. Match with employers in your trade.

scheme_name: Digital India Land Records Modernisation Programme
description: Digitization of land records to make them accessible online. Enables farmers and landowners to get certified land record copies quickly without visiting government offices.
eligibility: All landowners and farmers
benefits: Online access to land records, digital land record certificates, reduced harassment at government offices
application_process: Access through state land records portal (e.g., bhoomi.karnataka.gov.in, bhulekh.up.nic.in, e-dharti for Delhi)

ministry: Ministry of Women and Child Development
description: Maternity benefit scheme providing cash incentive to pregnant and lactating mothers for first live birth to compensate for wage loss and improve health and nutrition.
eligibility: All pregnant and lactating women for first living child. Not applicable to those already receiving maternity benefits under any law.
benefits: ₹5,000 in three installments: ₹1,000 on registration, ₹2,000 after first ANC, ₹2,000 after delivery and first immunization
application_process: Register at nearest Anganwadi centre or health facility with Aadhaar, bank account and MCP card

description: Poverty alleviation program mobilizing rural poor into self-help groups (SHGs). Provides skill training, financial inclusion, capacity building and interest subvention on loans.
eligibility: Rural BPL households, especially women. Focus on SC/ST, disabled, minorities, migrant workers.
benefits: SHG formation, revolving fund ₹10,000-15,000, community investment fund, interest subvention (7% for women SHGs), skill training and placement
application_process: Contact nearest Gram Panchayat or Block Mission Management Unit to join local SHG

scheme_name: PM Scholarship Scheme for Central Armed Police Forces
description: Scholarships for wards and widows of Central Armed Police Force personnel and Railway Protection Force who died in action or are disabled.
eligibility: Wards and widows of CAPF/RPF personnel. Students in first year of degree/diploma courses. Min 60% in qualifying exam.
benefits: ₹2,500/month for boys, ₹3,000/month for girls pursuing professional degree courses
application_process: Apply at scholarships.gov.in during application window (August-September typically)

description: Social security scheme for unorganized sector workers providing life and disability cover. Merged into PMJJBY but state-level versions continue.
eligibility: Unorganized sector workers aged 18-59 years, rural landless households, 48 identified occupational groups
benefits: ₹30,000 natural death, ₹75,000 accidental death/permanent disability, ₹37,500 partial disability, scholarship for 2 children studying Class 9-12
application_process: Enroll through nodal agency or CSC. Premium ₹200/year (shared by state and central government).

description: Health insurance for BPL workers in unorganized sector. Smart card based cashless health insurance for hospitalization at empanelled hospitals.
eligibility: BPL households, unorganized workers including construction workers, MGNREGA workers, domestic workers, rag pickers
benefits: ₹30,000 health cover per family per year, cashless hospitalization, ₹100 transport allowance per hospitalization
application_process: Enroll during annual enrollment drive through district administration

description: Provides free food grains (10 kg per month) to senior citizens aged 65+ who are eligible for old age pension but not receiving it.
eligibility: Senior citizens aged 65+ who are eligible for old age pension under NSAP but not receiving pension
benefits: 10 kg free foodgrains per month
application_process: Apply through state food department or district collector office

description: Credit-linked subsidy scheme for setting up micro-enterprises in non-farm sector. Provides margin money subsidy to generate employment in rural and urban areas.
eligibility: Any individual above 18 years. Minimum Class 8 pass for projects above ₹10 lakh. SHGs, charitable trusts, cooperative societies also eligible.
benefits: Subsidy 15-35% of project cost (higher for rural/SC/ST/women/ex-servicemen). Max project ₹50 lakh manufacturing, ₹20 lakh service sector.
application_process: Apply at kviconline.gov.in or nearest KVIC/KVIB/DIC office with project report

scheme_name: National Pension System (NPS) for Unorganized Sector
description: Voluntary retirement savings scheme open to all citizens. Flexible contribution, tax benefits, and portable across employers and locations.
eligibility: All Indian citizens aged 18-70. No minimum income requirement.
benefits: Tax deduction up to ₹1.5 lakh under 80C + additional ₹50,000 under 80CCD(1B). Market-linked returns. Partial withdrawal allowed.
application_process: Open NPS account at nearest Point of Presence (POP) - banks, post offices. Online at enps.nsdl.com

scheme_name: Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan / PM POSHAN (Mid Day Meal)
description: Free nutritious cooked meal to school children in government and government-aided schools from Class 1-8 to improve enrollment, attendance and nutrition.
eligibility: Children studying in Class 1-8 in government and government-aided schools
benefits: Free cooked mid-day meal every school day. Nutritional standards: 450-700 calories and 12-20g protein per meal.
application_process: Automatic for enrolled students in government schools. Enroll child at nearest government school.

description: Provides financial incentive for construction of household toilets in rural areas to achieve open defecation free status.
eligibility: Rural households without toilet. Priority to SC/ST, small and marginal farmers, landless laborers, women, differently-abled, AAY families.
benefits: ₹12,000 incentive for toilet construction paid in two installments after construction
application_process: Apply at Gram Panchayat or Block Development Office. Or apply through swachhbharatmission.gov.in

scheme_name: Jal Jeevan Mission - Har Ghar Jal
description: Provides tap water connection to every rural household. Functional household tap connections with adequate supply of safe drinking water.
eligibility: All rural households without functional tap water connection
benefits: Free tap water connection at home, assured supply of 55 litres per person per day
application_process: No application needed. Connect through Gram Panchayat. Register complaint if connection not provided at jalshakti-ddws.gov.in

ministry: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment
description: Provides assistive devices and equipment to disabled persons to improve their mobility, communication and daily living activities.
eligibility: Disabled persons from low income families. Income below ₹2 lakh/year. Disability certificate required.
benefits: Free assistive aids and appliances including wheelchairs, crutches, hearing aids, artificial limbs, DAISY players for visually impaired
application_process: Apply through district social welfare officer or ALIMCO distribution camps

description: Welfare fund scheme for farmers in Kerala providing pension, financial assistance for accidents and critical illness, and death benefits.
eligibility: Farmers who have cultivated land for at least one season in Kerala. Registration with Karshaka Kshemanidhi Board.
benefits: Monthly pension after 60 years, ₹1 lakh accident benefit, ₹50,000 critical illness benefit, ₹1 lakh death benefit
application_process: Register at nearest Krishi Bhavan with land documents. Apply at karshakakshemanidhi.kerala.gov.in

description: Integrated social security scheme for the destitute in Kerala. Provides comprehensive support including food, shelter, healthcare and livelihood to the most vulnerable.
eligibility: Destitute persons in Kerala — homeless, extremely poor families, abandoned elderly, single women, differently-abled without support
benefits: Monthly financial assistance, food security, housing assistance, healthcare support through community centers
application_process: Apply at local panchayat office or municipal office with income and destitution certificate

description: Monthly pension for agricultural laborers, unmarried women above 50, widows, and physically/mentally challenged persons in Kerala.
eligibility: Agricultural laborers above 60 years, widows/deserted women, physically disabled (40%+ disability), mentally challenged. Income below ₹1 lakh/year.
benefits: Monthly pension ₹1,600 for agricultural laborers, ₹1,400 for widows, ₹1,400 for disabled, ₹1,600 for unmarried women above 50
application_process: Apply at local panchayat/municipality with age, income, widow/disability certificate as applicable

description: Welfare fund for Non-Resident Keralites (NRKs) and return emigrants. Provides accident insurance, death benefits, pension and support for returning emigrants.
eligibility: Keralites who have worked abroad for at least one year. Registration with NORKA-ROOTS. Return emigrants who have come back.
benefits: ₹3 lakh accident insurance, ₹1 lakh death benefit, repatriation of mortal remains, ₹25,000 distress relief, pension for long-term members
application_process: Register at norkaroots.kerala.gov.in or NORKA district office

description: Women-led poverty eradication network through Neighborhood Groups (NHGs). Provides micro-finance, skill training, enterprise support and community development.
eligibility: Women from below poverty line families in Kerala. Membership through local Neighborhood Group.
benefits: Micro-loans at low interest, livelihood training, collective enterprise support, social development programs, government scheme linkage
application_process: Join nearest Kudumbashree Neighborhood Group through local panchayat or ward member

description: Programme for school students to develop civic sense, discipline and community service attitude through police-mentored training.
eligibility: Students of Class 8-12 in Kerala government and aided schools
benefits: Leadership training, personality development, traffic and disaster management training, direct employment preference in police recruitment
application_process: Enroll through school. Selection done by school and district police.

description: Livelihood Inclusion and Financial Empowerment (LIFE) mission to provide housing to homeless and landless families in Kerala.
eligibility: Landless homeless families, families living in dilapidated houses, Kudumbashree members without proper housing in Kerala
benefits: ₹4 lakh for house construction, land allotment for landless, regularization of encroachments
application_process: Apply through local body (panchayat/municipality) with income and housing certificate

scheme_name: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister's Comprehensive Health Insurance Scheme
ministry: Health and Family Welfare Department, Tamil Nadu
description: Health insurance scheme for low income families in Tamil Nadu providing cashless treatment for 1027 procedures including cancer, cardiac and neurosurgeries.
eligibility: Families with annual income below ₹72,000 in Tamil Nadu. Government employees not eligible.
benefits: ₹5 lakh health coverage per family per year, cashless treatment at 1000+ hospitals, covers 1027 procedures
application_process: Enroll using ration card at nearest government hospital or enrollment center

description: Provides affordable housing to SC/ST, BPL families and urban poor in Karnataka through various housing schemes.
eligibility: SC/ST families, BPL families, urban slum dwellers in Karnataka. Income below ₹3 lakh/year.
benefits: Housing units at subsidized rates, land allotment, construction subsidy up to ₹1.5 lakh
application_process: Apply at district Rajiv Gandhi Housing Corporation office or karenahousescheme.karnataka.gov.in

scheme_name: Maharashtra Mahatma Jyotirao Phule Jan Arogya Yojana
description: Health insurance scheme for below poverty line families in Maharashtra covering 996 procedures at network hospitals.
eligibility: Yellow and orange ration card holders in Maharashtra, farmers with crop loan under ₹1.5 lakh waiver
benefits: ₹1.5 lakh health coverage per family per year (₹2.5 lakh for cancer patients), cashless treatment at 1000+ hospitals
application_process: Enroll at government hospital using ration card

description: Investment support scheme for farmers in Andhra Pradesh providing ₹13,500 per year per farmer family combining central and state government support.
eligibility: All farmer families in Andhra Pradesh with cultivable land. Includes tenant farmers.
benefits: ₹13,500 per year (₹6,000 from PM Kisan + ₹7,500 from state) in two installments
application_process: Automatic for registered farmers. New registration at meeseva.gov.in or nearest Rythu Bharosa Kendra

ministry: Women and Child Development Department, Gujarat
description: Interest-free loan scheme for women SHGs in Gujarat to support income generating activities and financial empowerment of women.
eligibility: Women Self Help Groups in Gujarat with active bank account and good repayment record
benefits: Interest-free loan up to ₹1 lakh per SHG member (up to ₹100 crore sanctioned per SHG)
application_process: Apply through Women and Child Development office or Gram Panchayat

description: Universal health coverage scheme for all families in West Bengal providing cashless healthcare at empanelled hospitals. Smart card based.
eligibility: All families in West Bengal. Woman of the family is primary beneficiary and card holder.
benefits: ₹5 lakh health coverage per family per year, cashless treatment, covers pre and post hospitalization expenses
application_process: Enrollment at Gram Panchayat or municipality with family details. Swasthya Sathi smart card issued.

scheme_name: Rajasthan Mukhyamantri Chiranjeevi Swasthya Bima Yojana
description: Universal health coverage scheme in Rajasthan providing ₹25 lakh health insurance per family per year at empanelled hospitals.
eligibility: All families in Rajasthan. Free for NFSA, BPL, small farmers, EWS. Others pay ₹850/year premium.
benefits: ₹25 lakh health coverage per year, ₹5 lakh basic + ₹20 lakh catastrophic illness, cashless treatment at 2000+ hospitals
application_process: Register at chiranjeevi.rajasthan.gov.in or e-mitra center

description: Loan scheme for students in Bihar who have passed Class 12 to pursue higher education. Provides up to ₹4 lakh loan at very low interest rate.
eligibility: Students domiciled in Bihar who have passed Class 12 from recognized institution. For pursuing higher education in recognized institutions.
benefits: Loan up to ₹4 lakh at 4% interest (1% for women/transgender/disabled). No collateral, no guarantor.
application_process: Apply at 7nishchay-yuvaupmission.bihar.gov.in or nearest DRCC center

description: Marriage assistance scheme for girls from BC/SC/ST and minority communities in Telangana. Provides financial assistance for wedding expenses.
eligibility: Girls from BC (Kalyana Lakshmi), minority (Shaadi Mubarak) communities in Telangana. Bride should be 18+ and groom 21+.
benefits: ₹1,00,116 financial assistance transferred to bride's bank account for wedding expenses
application_process: Apply at meesevaseva.telangana.gov.in or nearest MeeSeva center after wedding registration

ministry: Women and Child Development Department, MP
description: Scheme to improve sex ratio and status of girl child in MP. Provides financial benefits to girl child from birth to higher education in multiple installments.
eligibility: Girl child born after January 1, 2006. Family should be registered under MP's Ladli Laxmi Yojana. Family should not be income tax payer.
benefits: Total benefit ₹1,43,000: ₹2,000/year for 5 years (NSC), scholarships at various education stages, ₹1 lakh at marriage after Class 12.
application_process: Register girl child at nearest Anganwadi or online at ladlilaxmi.mp.gov.in within 1 year of birth

description: Financial assistance for marriage of daughters from poor families in Punjab. One-time grant to reduce financial burden of wedding expenses.
eligibility: BPL families in Punjab with annual income below ₹32,790. Maximum 2 daughters per family.
benefits: ₹51,000 for marriage of daughter from BPL families
application_process: Apply at nearest Aam Aadmi Clinic or Social Security office with income certificate

description: Monthly financial assistance to women from economically weaker families in Assam to improve their livelihood and socioeconomic status.
eligibility: Women from families with income below ₹2 lakh/year in Assam. One woman per household.
benefits: ₹1,250 per month direct transfer to woman's bank account
application_process: Apply at nearest Arunodai center or through Assam government's online portal

description: Comprehensive livelihood and income augmentation scheme for farmers and landless agricultural laborers in Odisha.
eligibility: Small and marginal farmers, sharecroppers, landless agricultural laborers in Odisha
benefits: ₹10,000 per year for cultivation support (₹5,000 per season x 2), ₹12,500 for landless laborers, ₹2 lakh life insurance, ₹2 lakh accidental insurance
application_process: Apply at kalia.odisha.gov.in or nearest Common Service Centre

scheme_name: Jharkhand Savitribai Phule Kishori Samridhi Yojana
ministry: Women and Child Development Department, Jharkhand
description: Girls education and marriage assistance scheme for ST/SC/BPL families in Jharkhand. Provides financial support at different stages of girl's education.
eligibility: Girls from SC/ST/BPL/SECC-2011 listed/AAY ration card families in Jharkhand. Class 8-12 students.
benefits: ₹2,500 for Class 8-9, ₹5,000 for Class 10-12, ₹20,000 on completing Class 12 or on reaching 18 years for marriage/higher education
application_process: Enroll at school. Apply through school and district welfare office.

scheme_name: Himachal Pradesh Mukhya Mantri Swavalamban Yojana
description: Entrepreneurship promotion scheme in HP providing capital subsidy and interest subsidy for setting up enterprises by youth.
eligibility: HP domicile aged 18-45 years setting up enterprise. Manufacturing up to ₹1 crore, service/trade up to ₹25 lakh.
benefits: 25-35% capital subsidy on plant and machinery, 3% interest subsidy for first 5 years, machinery on deferred payment
application_process: Apply at District Industries Centre with project report and bank loan sanction letter
